# CLAUDE.md

Guidance for Claude Code when working in this repository.

## Project summary

OrioleDB is a PostgreSQL storage engine extension implemented primarily in C (`MODULE_big = orioledb` in `Makefile`), with SQL upgrade scripts and Python-based integration tests.

This project depends on a **patched PostgreSQL** build. Ensure `pg_config` from the patched PostgreSQL installation is in `PATH` before running `make`.

## Repository layout

See `doc/contributing/structure.mdx` for the repository file structure.

## Generated files and conventions

- `include/utils/stopevents_defs.h` and `include/utils/stopevents_data.h` are generated from `stopevents.txt` via `stopevents_gen.py` (triggered by `make`).
- `sql/*_prod.sql` (and `sql/*_dev.sql` when `IS_DEV=1`) are concatenated by `make` to produce corresponding `sql/*.sql` files (only for versions where a `*_prod.sql` source exists).
- `orioledb.typedefs` is generated by `typedefs_gen.py` and used by `pgindent`.

Prefer editing source inputs (`stopevents.txt`, `*_prod.sql`, `*_dev.sql`) rather than hand-editing generated outputs.

## Binary compatibility

See `doc/contributing/binary-compatibility.mdx` for the version variables in `include/orioledb.h` (`ORIOLEDB_BINARY_VERSION`, `ORIOLEDB_SYS_TREE_VERSION`, `ORIOLEDB_PAGE_VERSION`, `ORIOLEDB_COMPRESS_VERSION`) and cross-architecture caveats. When changing the on-disk page format, system tree format, compression format, or replication protocol, bump the relevant version and prefer data types with identical representation across architectures.
